signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Decision + Geopolitical Inflation + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD testing 0.7169 ahead of the RBA meeting where a 25bp hike to 4.35% is fully priced. Despite the 35bp carry advantage (RBA 4.10% vs Fed 3.75%), AUD is pressured by Middle East tensions and surging crude oil (+4.39%). COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 80% short creates a sharp institutional-retail divergence. If RBA delivers the hike and hawkish guidance, retail capitulation risk accelerates an upside move toward 0.7250; a 'dovish hike' risks a liquidation of crowded spec longs.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (specs 23% long) contradicts the RBNZ hawkish bias with a hike priced for the 27 May meeting. NZD is currently the G8 laggard (1W avg -0.383%) while USD maintains a +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%). This positioning extreme leaves NZD vulnerable to a short-squeeze if RBNZ rhetoric remains aggressive, targeting 0.5950. NZD/USD at 0.5873.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.710 vs norm of -0.154 (z=+2.01σ) signals a significant regime shift where EUR is trading as a pure risk proxy rather than a funding currency. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp USD carry lead) keeps the pair structurally capped at 1.1700. The decoupling from historical equity inverse correlation suggests EUR/USD downside will accelerate if STOXX 50 (-2.00%) weakness persists. EUR/USD at 1.1693.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.24 supported by 300bp Fed-BoJ carry (3.75% vs 0.75%). While JPY COT LF net short is crowded at -88,543, the pair remains bid as VIX at 18.3 (normal volatility) fails to trigger safe-haven JPY repatriation. 160.00 remains the critical watch level for BoJ verbal intervention; carry dominance persists unless VIX breaches 25 or the bear steepener regime (+56.6bp 2s10s) shifts to rapid curve inversion.
